ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #25, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Apr-28-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Patrick Larsen, via ZOOM, counsel for Wells Fargo Bank, Trustee.

At the outset, the court directs counsel to the several fees being requested in the petition. Counsel reports that the primary asset is a twelve-unit building which requires maintenance provided via property management and that the Trustee fee's that Wells Fargo is requesting are less than what they typically request to manage a trust of this size. The court reminds counsel that it is the court's obligation to determine if the requested fees are warranted under probate code. The court then advises counsel that a receipt for a painting job was never provided to the court. Counsel reports that a declaration was filed with the court which included, as Exhibit #1, a copy of the contract which outlines the total scope of the work being provided by the painting company. The court confirms receipt of the declaration with exhibit however, reminds counsel that no receipt was included.

After additional discussion, the 25th Accounting in this matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Tauscher. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #24, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-17-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Patrick Larsen for Wells Fargo (Trustee).

The Court raised concerns regarding the request for guideline fees by the Trustee and separately seeking fees for a property management company, and stated that these are basic duties of the Trustee and it is included in the guideline fees. The Court also raised the issue of the Attorney's fees being high for the preparation of three identical accountings noting that this is the 24th accounting and questioned the Attorney's billing practice and whether a quarter (.25) of an hour was billed to each trust, and how it can approve reimbursement for capital expenditure when no receipt was provided. Mr. Larsen stated that for nearly 25 years Wells Fargo has been managing these trusts and the property management fee has been paid in addition to the trustee's fees, that these trusts are complex, that there are sub-trusts, that each trust holds various interests in a multi-unit building that is income generating, that it is a benefit to the Beneficiaries to have a property management company, that it is a significant savings and the Trustee spends a significant amount of time managing the trust. The Court stated that it was not given any basis/time records to approve the request. Mr. Larsen stated that they maintain a call log, that once they hit 45 minutes for the three trusts, they then enter .25 for each trust and that it is typically accumulated in a short period of time, or they don't bill at all. Mr. Larsen also stated that the capital expense was for fire and life safety upgrades, and that he doesn't have an answer as to why there is no receipt. The Twenty Fourth Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #23, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-06-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Edward Watson for Wells Fargo Bank National Association.

Mr. Watson addressed fees stating that there are so many different departments (accounting/trust administration/investment arm, etc.), that they could not do time sheets for the last twenty three years and that they have relied on the 1% guideline safe harbor. Mr. Watson also stated that the Attorney did the petition, not the accounting, that they are always a month behind in reporting receipts and reimbursements, that the actual accounting is part of the Trustee's fees, that there is a learning curve and that they lost their Paralegal of long standing. In addition, Mr. Watson stated that their Associate had to handle the accounting, that a little more time was spent this year, that each Trust had different numbers, that it takes time, that rental deposits and rental reserves have to be reconciled, and that the Court Examiner's over time have changed what's required. All three Twenty Third Accounts and Reports of Trustee, etc. are taken under submission. Mr. Watson is to provide form of orders.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Tim Kneis. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.
